tldr: Bugsnag is a full hosted error monitoring product covering many platforms. The Sentry Native SDK only covers C/C++ and native apps and reports into Sentry. Migration only fits native apps moving into the Sentry ecosystem; non-native services need a separate plan.
what_to_export: Native project list and environment config
export_method: Identify which Bugsnag projects are native (C/C++) and in scope for the Sentry Native SDK; non-native projects are out of scope for this migration. Document those projects, environments, members, alerts, and integrations from the Bugsnag UI or its API. Capture symbol upload steps from your build pipeline as code. Save release tracking conventions. Treat API keys as values to rotate after cutover. If the Bugsnag API does not clearly expose a given setting, document it manually instead of guessing endpoints.
import_method: Create or reuse a Sentry organization and add Sentry projects for each native application. Integrate the Sentry Native SDK following the official Sentry documentation for native, including Crashpad or Breakpad backend selection. Replace Bugsnag native SDK initialization with sentry_init and equivalent configuration. Move symbol upload in your build pipeline to Sentrys debug file upload tooling. Configure releases and environments to mirror your Bugsnag conventions. Reconnect Slack, Jira, and ticketing integrations on the Sentry side. For non-native services that previously used Bugsnag, choose a separate path because the Sentry Native SDK does not cover them.
gotchas: Sentry Native SDK only covers C/C++ and native; plan separately for other platforms
Historical Bugsnag events do not transfer
Symbol upload workflow is non-trivial and must be set up carefully
Stability scores have no direct one-to-one in Sentry
Sentry pricing and ecosystem replace Bugsnags; review tier shape before commit

email.subject: Moving our native crash reporting off Bugsnag
email.body:
Hi team,

We are moving our native crash reporting to a SDK that fits better with the rest of our stack and plan to stop using Bugsnag for those projects. Our target timeline is 14 days from today.

During that period we will export project and integration configuration, document alert rules, and run the new SDK in parallel for validation. After cutover, the affected applications will stop sending events to Bugsnag.

Could you confirm the steps to disable auto-renewal, the effective end date under our current commitment, and any final invoicing details? Please share any recommended steps before account deactivation.

Thanks for the support.

Your customer
